commit.message:
Add quick start section after usecases

Signed-off-by: Codex (GPT 5) <codex@openai.com>

chore(tooling): add Entire and git hook integration files

Summary:
- Add Claude/Entire settings for local hook integrations
- Add additional git hooks under .githooks
- Add implement.md placeholder file

Rationale:
- Keep local agent and hook workflow configuration in repo state

Tests:
- not run (config and hook files only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(security): add pre-commit secret scanner

Summary:
- Add a repo-local pre-commit hook under .githooks/pre-commit
- Block staged commits containing common secret patterns, including
  Vercel app tokens (vca_/vcr_)
- Configure local git hooks path to .githooks for this clone

Rationale:
- Prevent accidental secret leaks before they reach commits and CI

Tests:
- ran .githooks/pre-commit with a staged test token and confirmed
  commit blocking behavior

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(security): remove leaked Vercel reference file

Summary:
- Remove docs/references/vercel-llms.txt from the repository

Rationale:
- Remove a file that triggered GitHub secret scanning for a leaked token

Tests:
- not run (file removal only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(skills): add Vercel workflow skills and references

Summary:
- Add deploy-to-vercel, vercel-cli, and web-design-guidelines skills
- Add Vercel CLI reference corpus and planning notes
- Update lock metadata and ignore rules for local workspace setup

Rationale:
- Capture reusable deployment workflows for multi-agent execution
- Keep Vercel command/reference material versioned in this repo

Tests:
- not run (docs/skills content update)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

phase7: add capability pressure audit + expand enum_select/measure_battery runtime semantics

Adds a TypeScript capability coverage audit tool that correlates bundled compiled profiles with runtime support-bundle diagnostics and ranks capability expansion pressure (with summary/markdown/json outputs).

Also extends node runtime specialized coercion for enum_select and measure_battery, updates policy-mode expectations, and adds regression tests across core + Homey app suites.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Refactor save pipeline and align version to 0.9.5

- Reorder save_items around materialized detail -> product -> snapshot -> c2c write

- Keep incremental detail rows while merging sparse payloads

- Keep item hydration async on access routes and normalize noface avatar

- Update tests to realistic non-empty detailDtoList payloads

- Bump app/api/footer version to 0.9.5 and document changes

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(repo): add initial docs and agent skill setup

Summary:
- Add repository guidance, workflow docs, and prompt references
- Add Codex/Claude skill definitions and Vercel React rules
- Add PR template and lock metadata for local agent tooling

Rationale:
- Capture the baseline repo structure needed to collaborate with agents
- Keep docs and skill artifacts versioned for reproducible setup

Tests:
- not run (initial content/bootstrap commit)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

homey/node-pairing: aggregate candidates across all connected bridges

Pivot node pairing discovery from single-bridge runtime resolution to all connected bridge sessions. The list now aggregates candidates per bridge, keeps deterministic ordering, annotates names when multiple bridges are active, and remains partial-failure tolerant per bridge.

Also adds harness coverage for multi-bridge aggregation and per-bridge failure tolerance.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs/plan: pivot node pairing to all-bridge aggregate discovery

- update sprint in-progress/next tasks to replace bridge-selector-first pairing with aggregate discovery across configured bridges

- update Phase 7 roadmap focus to include bridge-attributed, partial-failure-tolerant all-bridge node discovery

- sync architecture near-term direction with aggregate multi-bridge pairing model

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Improve fenced code block parsing, stricter command parsing (#324)

Now @ commands are only accepted if they are first thing on
a line (only whitespace may precede them).

Normalize AutoDoc input lines before parsing so stripping `#!`
in comment-mode  happens exactly once and fenced literals
stay literal in both source modes.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

🔧 config(deploy): 迁移 vercel 部署配置到 v1 CLI 方案

将项目的 vercel 部署入口切换为 `vercel-deploy-tool deploy`，
并新增根级 `vercel-deploy-tool.config.ts` 作为 v1 配置文件。

本次迁移同步改用 `defineConfig` 和 `vercelProjectName`，
保留现有部署目标，并兼容通过环境变量覆盖 Vercel 凭据。

commit.message:
homey: cascade-delete node devices when a bridge is removed

- add app runtime helper deleteNodeDevicesForBridge() to remove node devices bound to a bridgeId via manager/devices API

- wire bridge device onDeleted() to run cascade delete before tearing down bridge connection

- keep bridge deletion resilient: partial cascade failures are logged and bridge teardown still proceeds

- add app-runtime + bridge-device harness tests for cascade behavior and partial-failure reporting

- sync architecture and sprint docs with bridge lifecycle cascade semantics

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

homey: close phase 6 and polish settings diagnostics refresh UX

- extend settings runtime presenter with diagnostics refresh status/last success/last failure/last reason rows

- surface failure reason as runtime hint when no actionable recommendations are present

- add presenter regression coverage for diagnostics refresh rows and failure hint behavior

- mark Phase 6 complete and sync README/architecture/sprint/roadmap for Phase 7 kickoff

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

homey: surface per-bridge diagnostics refresh telemetry

- track bridge diagnostics refresh success/failure markers in app runtime and expose them via runtime diagnostics + bridge inventory APIs

- render refresh timestamps/reason in settings configured-bridges table and bridge tools runtime/summary panels

- add transition coverage to ensure refresh failure metadata clears after a later successful refresh

- sync architecture/API contract/sprint/roadmap docs for the telemetry slice

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

homey: fail open settings bootstrap when request gate script is missing

- replace hard bootstrap return with local in-page request-order gate fallback

- keep diagnostics/settings UI operational even when shared request-order gate asset is unavailable

- add guard assertions to prevent future fail-closed bootstrap regressions

- sync architecture and sprint notes for the remediation slice

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

homey: harden panel handler callback paths and event contracts

- extend panel liveness gate with callback-mode coverage across active pair/repair handlers

- add callback timeout/error propagation checks for stalled and invalid-action paths

- lock timed-session handler event inventory per driver to prevent panel contract drift

- sync architecture and sprint/roadmap docs for the hardening slices

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: productionize app with security, performance, and CI improvements

Summary:
- Enable Content Security Policy (CSP) in Tauri config (was null/disabled)
- Disable right-click context menu in production builds
- Wrap SortableTodoItem, ProgressBar, FilterTabs with React.memo
- Memoize derived state (filteredTodos, completedCount) with useMemo
- Enable logging in production builds at Warn level (was debug-only)
- Add lint and test jobs to CI workflow before platform builds
- Upload build artifacts per platform in CI
- Fill in Cargo.toml metadata (license, repo, description)

Rationale:
- CSP null is a critical security gap for a production Tauri app
- React.memo/useMemo reduce unnecessary re-renders as todo lists grow
- CI should gate on lint+test before expensive cross-platform builds
- Production logging at Warn level aids debugging without noise

Tests:
- vitest run: 30/30 passed
- eslint: clean
- vite build: success

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

新增吸积盘 V2 设计文档

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

homey: add panel liveness gate for pair and repair handlers

- add table-driven liveness contract tests for active panel events across bridge/node pair and repair flows

- assert response-shape expectations and per-handler invocation deadlines to catch spinner/hang regressions

- add timeout-behavior regression checks to ensure timed session wrappers reject stalled handlers

- sync architecture and sprint/roadmap docs for the new liveness gate coverage

commit.message:
homey: harden settings against stale diagnostics responses

- add request-order gate module for browser pages (settings/request-order-gate)

- ignore stale diagnostics/inventory responses when newer requests are in flight

- disable/re-enable Bridge Scope and Refresh controls deterministically during in-flight requests

- add request-order gate regression tests and timeout-guard assertions

- sync architecture and sprint/roadmap docs for this reliability slice

tools: add bridge-scoped flags to runtime smoke and support bundle CLIs

- add optional --bridge-id to homey:runtime-api:smoke and homey:support-bundle

- forward bridgeId to runtime diagnostics/support/recommendation routes where supported

- include bridge filter metadata in support-bundle exports (json/markdown source metadata)

- extend core tool tests for CLI parsing and query/body propagation

- sync README, API contract, architecture, sprint, and roadmap docs

repository.name: SimplyPages
repository.description: Simply Pages is a simple to the point server side rendering engine, with minimal web expereince required. Project has the simple goal of deconvoluting website deployment harnessing a grid based approach providing an extendable & composable component/module system with the option of performant dynamic rendering via minimal HTMX + JS

ffion: key outlook bundles to FFION_VERSION

Collapse the extra prompt-science version axis into Ffion itself.

- add a Ffion bundle registry and manifests keyed by FFION_VERSION
- preserve a historical Ffion 1.1.2 bundle and add the active 1.1.3 bundle
- update renderers, runners, QA helpers, and tests to use --ffion-version/--ffion-manifest
- remove the legacy science-registry files and bump Clyfar to 1.0.5 / Ffion to 1.1.3

- User requested dark mode, persistence, and testing

Rationale:

- Uses Tailwind CSS v4 + lucide-react (shadcn's icon library)

ci(release): Upload package artifacts for Craft

Pack all release targets on push and upload the generated tgz files as workflow artifacts so Craft can resolve revision artifacts.

This fixes release failures where artifact-provider/github reports no artifacts for the target revision.

Grant explicit checks/statuses read permissions to the release token so Craft can evaluate revision status reliably.

Run CI on release-oriented push branches so release automation commits produce check-runs instead of zero-context status.

chore(deps): bump non-breaking Go module dependencies (#1064)

### Motivation
- Keep the repository's Go dependencies current with non-breaking
minor/patch upgrades per the CONTRIBUTING workflow by running `go get -t
-u ./...`.
- Avoid forcing a toolchain upgrade by pinning `github.com/cli/cli/v2`
to `v2.86.0` so the module remains compatible with the repo Go `1.25.2`
baseline.
- Refresh transitive modules and checksums to reduce unexpected
build/linker issues from stale `go.sum` entries.

### Description
- Bumped multiple direct dependencies in `go.mod`, including
`buf.build/go/protovalidate` → `v1.1.3`, `github.com/go-git/go-billy/v5`
→ `v5.8.0`, `github.com/go-git/go-git/v5` → `v5.17.0`,
`github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` → `v0.44.1`,
`github.com/vektah/gqlparser/v2` → `v2.5.32`, the OpenTelemetry stack →
`v1.41.0`/`v0.66.0`, `golang.org/x/net` → `v0.51.0`, and
`google.golang.org/api` → `v0.269.0`.
- Refreshed many transitive module versions and checksums in `go.sum` to
reflect the updated module graph and added updated indirect entries for
modules such as `protoc-gen-validate`, `procfs`, and others.
- Explicitly retained `github.com/cli/cli/v2` at `v2.86.0` to avoid
requiring Go >= `1.25.7` in this repo's environment.

### Testing
- Ran `make install/dev` to install developer tooling, which completed
successfully.
- Ran `make lint` after tooling installation, which completed
successfully.
- Ran the test target (`export TZ=UTC; export TAGS="test_with_txtar";
make test`) and observed multiple test failures that appear to be
environment-dependent (version/tag assertions, missing external CLI
utilities used by some integration tests, and file-permission behavior
differences), so the full test suite did not pass in this environment.

------
[Codex
Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69aa1c8c46f88324834ab28c70611f3b)

feat: scaffold Tauri v2 app with React + Vite frontend

Summary:

commit.message:
feat(skills): 新增 codex-session-reader skill (#30)

## Why
- 需要一个仅面向 Codex 的只读 session/thread 阅读 skill，便于在已知 thread id 时直接查看会话内容。
- 通过 `codex app-server` 官方接口读取数据，可以避免耦合底层本地存储格式，并降低后续维护成本。

## What
- 新增 `codex-session-reader` skill，提供 `read` 子命令，通过 `codex app-server`
读取单个 Codex thread。
- 支持 `--preview-only` 与 0-based、接近 Python 切片语法的 `--turns` 参数，用于控制输出范围。
- 更新根目录 `README.md` 的 skills 表格，补充 `codex-session-reader` 与遗漏的
`coderabbit-cli` 条目。

## Testing
- `uvx ruff check
skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py --help`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
--help`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --preview-only`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns :5`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns -5:`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns 10:-1`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns 13`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns 1:10:2`（确认报错）

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

homey/pairing: stabilize list_devices dispatch with deterministic TS-only runtime

- remove custom onPair list handler wiring on bridge/node drivers and rely on SDK onPairListDevices flow

- disable allowJs in Homey app tsconfig to avoid mixed TS+JS source ambiguity at runtime

- refresh generated runtime JS for both drivers

- verified with npm --prefix co.lazylabs.zwavejs2homey test (19/19 passing)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(dashboard): build shared-types before next build for Vercel

The Vercel build fails because @code-reviewer/shared-types isn't
compiled before next build runs. The build script now navigates to
the monorepo root, builds the types package, then returns to run
next build.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
